texto: Situated on an island which I think it will one day cover, it rises like Venice from the sea, and like that fairest of cities in the days of her glory, receives into its lap tribute of all the riches of the earth.- Frances Trollope (1827)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Frances Trollope: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Situated on an island which I think it will one day cover, it rises like Venice from the sea, and…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, new-york-city, situated pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: Situated on an island which I think it will one day cover, it rises like Venice from the sea, and like that fairest of cities in the days of her glory, receives into its lap tribute of all the riches of the earth.- Frances Trollope (1827)

texto: A single word indicative of doubt, that any thing, or every thing, in that country is not the very best in the world, produces an effect which must be seen and felt to be understood. If the citizens of the United States were indeed the devoted patriots they call themselves, they would surely not thus encrust themselves in the hard, dry, stubborn persuasion, that they are the first and best of the human race, that nothing is to be learnt, but what they are able to teach, and that nothing is worth having, which they do not possess.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Frances Trollope sobre temas ligados a educacao. A afirmação “A single word indicative of doubt, that any thing, or every thing, in that country is not the ver…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, america, americans, útil em momentos de americans e united-states.
